register record listener before start reading

There is a possible race condition where replay state machine misses a notification
because new record is available after reader attempts to read it and before the
listener is registered. To avoid this it should register the listener before
it attempts to read from logstream.

Make the code more safe

validate s3 config by creating a throwaway S3BackupStore at startup

If configuration like region, endpoint and credentials are not configured in zeebe config and not available from the environement, creating S3BackupStore fails. S3BackupStore is created during role transitions. Then it will fail the transition, and the partition goes unhealthy. To fail fast, when the configurations are not available, we create a throw away S3BackupStore while validating the configs.

remove handling of invalid variable

* remove the explicit handling of the case that the variables are not valid JSON from the processor
* the variables are already verified in the gateway
* even if invalid variables would be passed to the processor then the stream processor would catch the exception and reject the command is a similar way

do not use `em.merge()` internally

Every managed entity did pass thru `em.merge()` originally. While it was a good invariant
for managed entities, it turns out the method was overcomplicated and had a serious performance
impact on the persistence.

With this change, UoW won't use `em.merge()` during flush operation. Instead, entity factory is
used directly to create new instance and new method - `uow.registerManaged()` is used to add it
to the identity map.

The method `em.merge()` is still present and used is some parts of the ORM like in `assign` helper,
it is also still a valid goto method for loading entities from cache.

Add `fetch::Arguments::is_stateless()` to aid proper use of arguments.

When arguments are used, haves are reset every round in stateless protocols, while
everything else is repeated. However, this also means that previously confirmed common
commits aren't repeated unless this is specifically implemented by the user of `Arguments`.

That caller can now easily determine if negotiations have to be compensated for.

Please note that `Arguments` explicitly doesn't implement repeating of all prior arguments, which
would also repeat a lot of *in-vain* haves.

Add support for optionally logging Flux queries

New configuration:

```toml
[http]
flux-log-enabled = true
```

will produce log entries similar to:

```
ts=2019-01-11T14:47:46.124347Z lvl=info msg="Executed Flux query" log_id=0CwLysq0000 service=httpd compiler_type=flux response_size=1467 query="from(bucket:\"test\") |> range(start: -5000h) |> limit(n:5)" stat_total_duration=3.949ms stat_compile_duration=3.183ms stat_queue_duration=0.026ms stat_plan_duration=0.055ms stat_requeue_duration=0.000ms stat_execute_duration=0.668ms stat_max_allocated=3200 stat_concurrency=1
```

pull setter calls into evaluation methods

The setter call on the evaluation result is tied to the field being evaluated. In order to avoid any errors,
the setter call on EvalResult is pulled into the evaluation method that needs it.
